# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build Workflow with Shell Commands (CI/CD)
on: [push] # or [workflow_dispatch], based on when you want your Actions run locally, change accordingly in .github/workflows/*  file.
jobs:
  <job-id>: // ID of the job(required). Available from v3.5 (manual) after a workflow is defined with `v2`. Default can be filled automatically for new jobs by names and branches, default to 'defaults' if no other value provided manually during configuration or when this field not set at all in an existing file
  runs-on: // specify the type of Linux distribution that should run your job e.g., Ubuntu-latest , macOS latest versions can be used with appropriate Docker images as per requirements, defaults to matrix['ubuntu20.04', 'macOS-latest'] which means using these two OS and their corresponding docker image for running build
  steps: # The list of actions that run on your job runner(s). Each item in the array specifies a step action (e.g., "run", if you want to execute any commands or executables) with some settings/arguments, see more details at https://github.com/actions/stdenv
    - name: Checkout code // This is where we check out our repository using `git clone` syntax in the context of this job (not required as it's handled by dependencies if you have them set up)  Default to a shallow copy from 'master'. It’ll not include any git data, just history and blob contents. Remove .git* files on your checkout step
      uses: actions/checkout@v2 // Dependency of the previous action; this sets repository name (optional), token (required) if you have an authorized one in a github secret `my-github-token`  and directory, default to '.'. This means that current git repo's files will be cloned into it with shallow copy or depth set based on '.git*'.
    - name: Run Custom Build Command // Executes the custom build commands provided as input by user (passed in workflow) during each step of job run, use these 'steps'-like inputs to control your process. Evaluated expressions and substitutions will be performed within this context e.g., "echo ${{ jobs.exampleJob }}".
      env: // Environment variables that are available for the steps or runs in `jobs` as keys with their defaults if not set manually, see more details at https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments#setting-up-your-runners and also can be passed via workflow inputs e.g., name: "VARIABLE_NAME", value (required).
      run: // Main command or script to execute as a step of your job, supports substitution `${{ jobs["exampleJob"] }}` in it's context etc(it should return an exit status indicating how the task executed - 0 for success/non-zero if fail) and any output text.
      shell: // The type or path to execute on run environment (Default is bash). Affected actions are "run", so make sure you can also call it as a step's action, e.g., `shell` instead of just plain string commands in the same line ie.: name:"Installing dependencies". Then: shell-cmd:'npm install', if not set default to 'bash'.
